page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 30.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix multiplication and cryptography. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

In benchmark testing, the c4-standard-8 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance. It achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression (34.68 GB/sec), single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.84 GB/sec), and Geekbench single-core testing (2203.0). Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 8381.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 8425.22. Memory read bandwidth peaks at 668.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks but drops to 61.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and text processing. It performs efficiently on small-scale language model tasks, achieving a strong text generation speed of 256.7 tokens/sec with a 135M parameter model. However, it exhibits weak performance on larger models, dropping to 7.18 tokens/sec for text generation with a medium 7B model, and lacks the capacity for large 70B models. This makes it a practical choice for standard application hosting, database operations, and lightweight inference, while heavier computational tasks or large-scale AI workloads require GPU-accelerated or memory-optimized alternatives.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) and 30.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.3 GHz, it features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In performance testing, it delivers top-tier single-threaded capabilities, notably in gzip compression (34.68 GB/sec) and Geekbench single-core execution (2203.0). Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 8381.0. This resource profile is suitable for general-purpose computing, software compilation, and lightweight text processing. However, it is constrained for larger AI workloads, showing weak performance on medium-sized LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 30 GB RAM. It excels in single-threaded tasks but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded performance.
tagline: A dedicated eight-vCPU virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for balanced general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz
30.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB per core
Top-tier single-threaded performance in compression and Geekbench tests
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for hardware acceleration
No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for general-purpose application hosting, text processing, and light inference
